description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a distinctive ribbed appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a matte black finish that contrasts with the lighter, slightly speckled stone of the grooved top. The block is thick and robust, suggesting it is designed to serve as a substantial barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The overall construction appears monolithic, with no visible seams or joints. There are no visible signs of weathering, markings, or engravings on any surface.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, solid black sides. The top is ribbed and speckled, giving it a striking striped appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running away from the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the horizontal grooves and the solid, smooth sides.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the length and depth of the grooves as they recede from the viewer.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and unworn
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
